Package application.view

Class GameView

java.lang.Object
application.view.View
application.view.GameView
public class GameView extends View
The main view class for the gameplay screen.

This class orchestrates the layout of the game grid, the level information sidebar, and various overlays (tooltips, win screens). It also listens for global game events to trigger visual updates or transition the game state.

  • Constructor Details

    • GameView

      public GameView(GameLevel level)
      Constructs a new GameView for the specified level. Initializes the UI hierarchy including the grid, inventory pane, and back button. * @param level The game level data to be rendered.

  • Method Details

    • updateTileAndAdjacent

      public void updateTileAndAdjacent(GridPos pos)
      Forces a visual refresh of a tile and its four immediate neighbors. Often used when a tile placement affects the adjacency logic of surrounding movers. * @param pos The center grid position to update.

    • startup

      public void startup()
      Called when the view becomes active. Resets the engine and starts game music.
      Specified by:
      startup in class View

    • cleanup

      public void cleanup()
      Called when navigating away from this view. Unregisters event listeners and cleans up UI components to ensure resources are freed.
      Specified by:
      cleanup in class View

    • updatePoints

      public void updatePoints(RenderEvent ev)
      Handles batch visual updates based on render events.
      Parameters:
      ev - The event containing a list of grid points that changed during a tick.

    • showGameWinOverlay

      public void showGameWinOverlay(GameWinEvent event)
      Pauses the game and triggers the "Win" animation overlay. The overlay drops from the top of the screen with a custom bounce effect. * @param event The win event containing completion data.

    • getLevelInfoPane

      public LevelInfoPane getLevelInfoPane()
      Returns:
      The pane containing level statistics and inventory.

    • getTooltipLayer

      public TooltipLayer getTooltipLayer()
      Returns:
      The layer responsible for rendering tooltips over components.

    • getInstance

      public static GameView getInstance()
      Returns:
      The current static instance of the game view.

    • setInstance

      public static void setInstance(GameView view)
      Parameters:
      view - Sets the global static instance of this view.
